Its extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, cough, and skin infections. intro: Mitracarpus fischeri is a plant species, also known as "Majá" in some regions, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. Traditionally, Mitracarpus fischeri has been used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Mitracarpus fischeri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Mitracarpus fischeri is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of tinctures or capsules, often marketed for its purported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
